title: ISO 14971 Risk Record
description: Records the risk presented by one hazardous situation — a hazard's realization in a specific circumstance — including its estimated severity/probability and the residual-risk acceptance decision after risk control. Complements crates/trustsc-governance/src/lib.rs::Hazard (referenced via hazard_ref), which models the hazard's identity and controlling requirement(s) but not the hazardous-situation, severity/probability, or residual-risk detail ISO 14971 distinguishes. See docs/iso14971/02-risk-analysis-and-evaluation.md §5.4 and docs/governance/citation-convention.md.

risk_id.description: e.g. 'RK-001'. Sequential, unique across the risk management file.

hazard_ref.description: The id of the Hazard (docs/iec62304/schemas/hazard.schema.json) this risk record specializes into a specific hazardous situation. A single hazard may give rise to more than one risk record.

hazardous_situation.description: The specific circumstance in which the referenced hazard's potential is actually realized (ISO 14971:2019 §3 Terms and definitions distinguishes 'hazard' from 'hazardous situation') — e.g. 'clinician relies on the sedation-index display during a delayed-update window'.

severity.description: ISO 14971:2019 §5.5 Estimation of risk. Manufacturer's own judgment; not computed by TrustSC.

probability.description: ISO 14971:2019 §5.5 Estimation of risk. Manufacturer's own judgment; not computed by TrustSC.

risk_control_measures.description: ISO 14971:2019 §7.1-§7.3 Risk control. Ideally naming real trustsc_governance::RequirementId values (crates/trustsc-governance/src/lib.rs) rather than free-standing mitigation notes, since Hazard.controlled_by already requires this link at the Hazard level.

residual_risk_acceptable.description: ISO 14971:2019 §7.4/§6 Risk evaluation. A single field covering both pre-control risk evaluation (§6) and post-control residual risk evaluation (§7.4) — a deliberate simplification; see docs/iso14971/02-risk-analysis-and-evaluation.md §6 for why the schema does not model the standard's full pre/post-control state machine.
